steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what 'mastery' means as used in the text. The context: the breezes 'meet and conflict as though each strove for the mastery of the air.' The breezes are competing — each is fighting to gain control over the other. The word must fit a struggle between two opposing forces.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Domination': Control or supremacy over another — exactly what each breeze is striving to achieve by overpowering the other. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Familiarity': Describes knowledge or acquaintance, not a contested outcome. Breezes don't fight for familiarity. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Skillfulness': Describes ability or expertise, not dominance over an opponent. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Comprehension': Describes understanding, which has nothing to do with breezes competing for control. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
